data.title: Target Stock (NYSE:TGT) Notches Up as it Runs Into ICE Troubles in Minneapolis
data.description:
It would be easy to think that only Home Depot (HD) is seeing issues connected to immigration enforcement of late. But that is not so, as retail giant Target (TGT) is facing troubles of its own. Customers are calling on the store chain to keep ICE operations out, putting it between a rock and a hard place. The news actually hit shareholders fairly well, who gave shares a fractional boost in the closing minutes of Wednesday's trading.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Officials from the Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agency reportedly detained two Target employees in a store vestibule following reports of "verbal sparring" in the parking lot. One of the employees shouted, "I'm literally a U.S. citizen!" This did not stop said officials from hustling said employee into a van.

Both Target employees were found to be American citizens, and both were later released, though the Department of Homeland Security noted that one individual had been "…arrested for assaulting a federal law enforcement offers (sic)." The video in turn fired up outcry from parts of the general public, who followed up with protests outside stores. Target, in turn, responded in about the only way it could: new corporate training programs about what to do when interacting with immigration officials.

Exposed Source Code

This was not the end of Target's problems, but rather, only the beginning. Target also found itself exposed in the source code department, with several Gitea repositories emerging that contained Target internal code and developer documentation. It is unclear who was responsible for this breach, but Target employees reportedly confirmed that the code was genuine.

Customer data was safe, reports suggest, at least as far as this breach went. But the loss of source code safety represents a potentially much larger incident in the making. SecurityScorecard chief information security officer Steve Cobb noted, "Unlike breaches that focus on customer data, a compromise of development infrastructure exposes the blueprints of how a company's systems operate. Once engineering assets surface publicly, even briefly, the spread becomes extremely difficult to contain and can create opportunities for deeper compromise."

Is Target Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TGT stock based on eight Buys, 13 Holds and four Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 9.87% loss in its share price over the past year, the average TGT price target of $98.83 per share implies 9.88% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: OpenAI Signs $10B Deal with Chip Startup Cerebras for More Computing Power
data.description:
AI firm OpenAI has signed a large new deal with chip startup Cerebras Systems to secure more computing power for ChatGPT and its other AI tools. Under this agreement, OpenAI will use Cerebras-designed chips and buy up to 750 megawatts of computing capacity over the next three years, which The Wall Street Journal says is worth more than $10 billion. This comes as OpenAI now serves more than 900 million users every week and is running into serious limits on the amount of computing power it can access. It is also worth noting that OpenAI CEO Sam Altman is a personal investor in Cerebras, and the two companies first discussed working together back in 2017.

data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Worker Suspended After Trump Heckling, Ford Stock (NYSE:F) Slips
data.description:
Remember how legacy automaker Ford (F) was playing host to President Trump recently? Well, Trump took a tour around the plant, past some of the workers. Normally, that is a good time to keep your head down and your eyes on your work. But someone decided to weigh in, publicly, and get suspended for it. The resulting fracas sent Ford shares down fractionally in Wednesday afternoon's trading.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The worker in question saw what he apparently believed was an opportunity to take Trump to task over the Jeffrey Epstein case, declaring that Trump was a "pedophile protector," among other, less audible things. President Trump responded to this overture with a familiar gesture known commonly as "the bird."

The White House, via representative Steven Cheung, noted, "A lunatic was wildly screaming expletives in a complete fit of rage, and the President gave an appropriate and unambiguous response." The United Auto Workers, meanwhile, noted that one of its members had indeed been suspended, and said nothing more beyond that. Ford itself declared that the incident was "…a very small part of the day," and most Ford employees were "…great."

Truck of the Year, Again

Meanwhile, the Ford Maverick Lobo managed to land a familiar honor once more. For the sixth time in a row, the Ford Maverick Lobo was declared the North American Truck of the Year. This has, reports note, never actually happened before, making the Ford Maverick Lobo a standout in its field for a completely different reason.

Ford vehicle line program director Pam Wylie noted, "The Maverick Lobo isn't just a new offering; it's a response to a movement. We saw our customers personalizing their trucks for on-road performance and style, and we decided to give them a factory-tuned option that carries the Built Ford Tough seal of approval. This award is a tribute to our customers who continue to inspire us every day."

Is Ford Stock a Good Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on F stock based on three Buys, 11 Holds and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 40.5% rally in its share price over the past year, the average F price target of $13.77 per share implies 0.46% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: CrowdStrike's (CRWD) CEO Calls AI a 'Drunken Intern' amid Acquisition Spree
data.description:
George Kurtz, the CEO of cybersecurity firm CrowdStrike (CRWD), recently warned that giving AI agents too much freedom inside corporate systems is like "giving full access to a drunken intern," because no one knows what they might do. In an interview on Yahoo Finance's Opening Bid, he said that companies need stronger tools to control how both people and AI access sensitive systems. That concern helps explain why CrowdStrike is moving quickly to build what Kurtz calls a next-generation identity and security platform.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In fact, CrowdStrike announced on Monday that it is buying browser security firm Seraphic Security for about $420 million. This comes just one week after it agreed to acquire SGNL for $740 million in order to improve how organizations manage human and AI identity access through its Falcon platform. These moves also build on 2025 acquisitions, which include AI security firm Pangea for $260 million and data startup Onum for $290 million. Taken together, these deals are designed to increase control over AI agents and users before they can create security problems.

At the same time, CrowdStrike's core business is already benefiting from growing demand for cybersecurity. In its latest quarter, revenue rose by 22% to $1.23 billion, while annual recurring revenue climbed 23% to $4.92 billion, and non-GAAP operating income reached a record $264.6 million. For the current quarter, the company expects sales to come in between $1.29 billion and $1.3 billion, with earnings per share of $1.09 to $1.11.

Is CrowdStrike Stock a Good Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Buy consensus rating on CrowdStrike stock based on 24 Buys, 11 Holds, and zero Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average CrowdStrike price target of $567.21 per share implies 23.6% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: 10-Year Treasury Yield Sinks on Elevated Safe-Haven Demand
data.description:
The 10-year Treasury yield is down by 3.5 bps on Wednesday to 4.136% amid rising safe-haven demand spurred by elevated geopolitical tensions. The yield on Treasuries moves in the opposite direction of their prices.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Growing unrest in Iran has captured the attention of President Trump, who said earlier this week that "help is on its way" for protesters. That could mark the beginning of American involvement in another Middle Eastern conflict, although Trump hasn't specified how he will address the situation yet.

Stock Market Volatility Leads to Demand for Treasuries

Furthermore, both the S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) have pulled back in recent days, depressing investors' appetite for risk and shifting capital into safer assets, like bonds and commodities.

An inflation report from the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) this morning contributed to the market decline, showing Producer Price Index (PPI) rising by 3% annually, above the consensus estimate of 2.7%. While elevated inflation typically pushes yields higher because investors demand greater compensation for lost purchasing power, safe-haven demand dominated on Wednesday, sending yields lower.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: "Apple Went In": Intel Stock (NASDAQ:INTC) Gains as Trump Spills Beans on Apple Investment
data.description:
Has Apple (AAPL) invested in chip stock Intel (INTC)? If you believe recent remarks made by no less than a sitting president, yes, it has. President Trump recently mentioned as much during a press event, though some wonder if, perhaps, the mention was a mistake instead. The idea that Apple might have bought in on Intel stock was enough for other investors to flood in, and Intel shares gained over 3% in Wednesday afternoon's trading.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Trump recounted the story to a reporter who asked if the United States would be buying any more shares of Intel. While Trump was unsure if there would be more investment in Intel, he did note there would likely be more investment in other companies. The idea of a United States sovereign wealth fund has been advanced before, after all.

But then, Trump noted, "As soon as we went in, Apple went in, Nvidia (NVDA) went in, a lot of smart people went in—they followed us. So, yeah, we're doing that deal." That Nvidia went in was well-known already, but that Apple went in was much more of a surprise to the outside viewer. There were some reports earlier suggesting that Apple had taken an interest, but word about Apple actually signing checks was less available.

Watching the Celestials

Meanwhile, Intel's upcoming line of graphics processing units, the Celestial lineup, is catching attention. The Battlemage line did a good job of paving the way, and now, the upcoming Celestials are especially closely watched.

Specifically, reports suggest that the Xe3P iGPU could be a significant boost in capability for the Nova Lake lineup. In fact, the combination of Nova Lake and the Xe3P iGPU could actually mean better graphics performance than the integrated graphics found on the Panther Lake lineup. Most of these projections are early word, generated mainly by leakers. The Nova Lake line will not arrive until 2027, reports note. Still, the early word is promising, and exciting enough to merit further consideration.

Is Intel a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on INTC stock based on eight Buys, 17 Holds and five Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 139.81% rally in its share price over the past year, the average INTC price target of $41.73 per share implies 13.89% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Boeing Stock (NYSE:BA) Sinks Thanks to New Competitor
data.description:
For years, the commercial aviation market has been mostly split between aerospace stock Boeing (BA) and its immediate rival Airbus (EADSY). But there are some signs that this duopoly may be about to break up, and one new competitor just landed a hefty investment to bring the fight to these two titans. The news did not sit well with Boeing investors, and shares slid modestly in Wednesday afternoon's trading.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The new competitor is known as JetZero, and is located in Long Beach. It recently landed $175 million in a Series B financing round, raising the total amount of capital behind it—including government grants and commercial commitments—to a little over $1 billion. With that cash in hand, reports note, JetZero will start building a jet that looks vaguely like a manta ray, but offers some incredible developments in plane capability.

The JetZero plane—known as the Z4—will offer a 50% fuel savings when compared to standard models like Boeing's 767, reports note. The plane is expected to take its initial flight before the end of 2027, and JetZero hopes that its alternative in mid-range jets will prove welcome to airlines.

Surging Delivery Numbers

JetZero will have its work cut out for it, though, as Boeing sales and deliveries continue to add up to impressive numbers. Boeing delivered 63 airplanes in December, reports noted, which gets Boeing up to fully 600 jets delivered for its entirety of 2025. Of those 63, 45 were 737s, and 18 were widebody jets.

And airlines continued to place orders. Even without President Trump making the rounds and making deals accordingly, Boeing still brought in 174 orders for the month, leaving aside cancellations or jets taken from reserve. The model breakdown features 140 737 jets ordered, and 35 widebody jets ordered.

Is Boeing a Good Stock to Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on BA stock based on 14 Buys, two Holds and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 47.14% rally in its share price over the past year, the average BA price target of $253 per share implies 4.46% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Google Stock (GOOGL) Slips despite New Personal Intelligence Feature
data.description:
Shares of tech giant Google (GOOGL) are down today despite the rollout of a new Gemini AI feature that uses Gmail, Search history, Photos, and YouTube activity to give more personalized answers. This update, called Personal Intelligence, is meant to make Gemini more helpful by understanding what users like, do, and need. Unsurprisingly, Google executive Josh Woodward said that this is a big step toward making Gemini more personal and powerful. For now, the feature is launching in beta in the United States to Google AI Pro and AI Ultra subscribers.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

At the same time, Google is trying to reduce privacy concerns by making Personal Intelligence completely optional. Users have to turn it on themselves and can choose which apps Gemini is allowed to access. For example, someone could let Gemini use their email and photos but block their search history. This approach gives Google a strong advantage over rivals like OpenAI, because Google already has access to far more personal data, even though ChatGPT can now remember some details from past chats.

However, what makes this update different is that Gemini can now use this information automatically, without being asked, whenever it thinks it will improve an answer. For instance, if someone asks about buying new car tires, Gemini could already know what type of car they drive and give more accurate suggestions. Still, Google admits that the system is not perfect and may sometimes overreach or misunderstand details. As a result, users can turn off personalization for any reply, give feedback, or correct Gemini directly.

Is GOOGL Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on GOOGL stock based on 27 Buys and seven Holds assigned in the past three months. Furthermore, the average GOOGL price target of $340.45 per share implies 1.6% upside potential.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Fed's Paulson Says Trump's Tariffs Haven't Led to 'Broader Inflation'
data.description:
Federal Reserve Bank of Philadelphia President Anna Paulson doesn't believe that President Trump's tariffs have contributed to rising prices and reiterated her support for interest rate cuts later this year if the job market stabilizes and inflation drifts toward the central bank's target of 2%.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"We've seen progress on underlying inflation that is likely to continue and there is no evidence to date that tariff-induced price increases are leading to broader inflation," she said in prepared remarks for an economic event in Philadelphia on Wednesday. "In addition, long-term inflation expectations are anchored at levels consistent with a gradual return to 2 percent inflation."

Paulson Says Restrictive Rates Help Ease Inflation

Paulson believes that current interest rates are "a little restrictive," which will help guide inflation lower. Her base case in 2026 is for economic growth of 2%, falling inflation, and a stabilizing labor market. However, she highlighted that the effects of AI and deregulation could affect both inflation and employment.

Paulson acknowledged rising labor market risks, which influenced her to support 75 bps of rate cuts last year. The Fed is set to meet for its first Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meeting of 2026 on January 28, where it is expected to hold rates steady.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Reports Claim Anthropic and OpenAI Are Preparing to go Public
data.description:
Multiple media reports claim that artificial intelligence (AI) start-up companies Anthropic and OpenAI are taking steps to hold initial public offerings (IPOs) as early as this year.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The New York Times (NYT) was the first to report that Anthropic and OpenAI have taken the early steps toward going public, joining Elon Musk's SpaceX in what could become a blockbuster year for technology IPOs on Wall Street.

According to the reports, Anthropic and OpenAI have each taken the preliminary steps to hold an IPO in coming months. SpaceX, a satellite and commercial space company, is also moving towards an IPO this year in what could be the biggest market debut ever for a U.S. concern.

Massive Valuations for Anthropic and OpenAI

These three companies would rank among the most valuable to ever go public since Saudi Aramco's 2019 debut, which valued the energy giant at $1.7 trillion. Anthropic is currently in funding talks that could value it at $350 billion, while OpenAI carries an estimated worth of $500 billion. SpaceX was most recently valued at $800 billion.

The potential public offerings of these tech giants could create what some are calling an "IPO supercycle" or "monster listing mania," setting up a watershed moment for Silicon Valley and the artificial intelligence sector.

If all three companies proceed with their plans, 2026 could mark a historic year for high-profile tech listings on public markets.
data.author: Joel Baglole
